Welcome back to Below the Surface. I'm joined by Marco Silva, technical diver and instructor. Marco, thanks for surfacing for us. Ah, apretto, rila. This episode is brought to you by Suunto. Suunto builds dive computers and outdoor watches. Get started at suunto.com slash podcast. New divers think planning is paperwork. You call it survival. Dive planning is risk management, plain and simple. It's the gas you don't use and the acid you don't rush. The incident I heard is the one nobody knows about, so we'll name them all first. And you plan the whole dive one system, depth, gas, time, cure, because the accident we've been told doesn't interact, nor in any single number. And we'll review the ad code of incident report, the pattern every time. So it's not so different from managing any kind of risk. No different at all. A dive plan and a portfolio are the same discipline, system of collateral risk, or the one you can describe in advance is the one you can actually control. Thanks to Patty for supporting the show. Patty is the global standard for dive education. Thanks, rila. That's it for this Below the Surface. Dive safe.
